name: Baked Corn Empanaditas
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Finely-ground deep yellow masa harina
1 large Tomato, peeled, seeded and finely diced
1/2 pound Potatoes, cooked, cut into 1/4 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
Combine the masa harina, water and salt in a large mixing bowl. Stir until smooth. The dough should be slightly sticky and form a ball when pressed together. Flatten a small ball of dough between your palms. If the edges crack, add water to the dough, about one tablespoon at a time, until a test piece does not crack. Cover with a damp towel until ready to use.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over moderate heat. Add the onion and saute until soft, 3 to 5 minutes. Add the garlic, jalapeno, cumin, salt and pepper and saute 1 minute longer to release the aromas. Add the tomato, potatoes and corn and cook, stirring occasionally, until the liquid from the tomatoes is absorbed (about 2 to 3 minutes). Remove from heat, stir in the parsley and cheese and correct the seasoning. Set aside to cool.
Divide the dough into 24 golf ball-sized pieces. If necessary, moisten your hands with water to prevent sticking. Flatten each piece into a 3-inch disk. Place a heaping teaspoon of stuffing on the empanada and close by pressing the edges together with your fingers. Shape into a half moon. Repeat until all halfmoons are formed.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and arrange the empanaditas on top. Brush with the glaze and prick each with a fork to make steam holes. Bake for about 25 to 30 minutes, or until golden. Remove and transfer to a rack to cool slightly. Serve warm.
This recipe yields 4 to 6 servings.
